sections.markdown:
- 4 km easy run
- Keep it short and relaxed
- Treat this as support for consistency, not a quality session

- Apple Health recorded `242` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `146.6 bpm`, ranging from `78.0` to `171.5 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `129.1 bpm` and second-half average HR was `164.0 bpm` (+34.9 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `158.9 spm`, with a working range of `117.3` to `174.0 spm` after excluding `12` start-stop outliers below `100 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `150.6 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `164.9 spm` (+14.3 spm), which suggests turnover lifted rather than falling away.

- Strava moving pace averaged `6:18 /km` across `7.031 km` in `44:16` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `7:08 /km` and second-half pace was `5:28 /km` (-100 s/km), so pace picked up through the second half.

- Strava summary HR came in at `147.7 bpm` average and `173 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `133.4 bpm` in the first half and `165.6 bpm` in the second (+32.2 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Moving time was `44:16` out of `46:01` elapsed (96.2%), which shows only minor interruption cost.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `11` stop segments and `1.4%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `69.2 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `4.2 m` and `15.4 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `33.0 m` in the first half and `36.2 m` in the second, so the climbing load was split fairly evenly.
